title: After-Action Review — March 7, 2026
scope: Mobile Compatibility Fix and WVL State Persistence Migration.

rationale.item: The app was unusable on mobile devices: WVL owner mode's 7 navigation tabs overlapped into an unreadable jumble on mobile screens, the hero banner overflowed, and every tab bar in the app (Pro Leagues, Play, International, DraftyQueenz) lacked mobile scroll support.

WVL mode lost state mid-season, making it unplayable: the WVLMultiTierSeason object had no to_dict()/from_dict() serialization, NiceGUI's storage layer couldn't JSON-serialize it, so it lived only in server memory; any page reload, server restart, or session timeout lost the entire season.

reconnect_timeout=30.0 in main.py meant that locking a phone screen, switching apps, or briefly losing cellular signal for more than 30 seconds would drop the WebSocket session and destroy all in-memory state.

Pre-existing serialization bug: serialize_pro_league_season() omitted the ties and div_ties fields from ProTeamRecord, so tie records were silently zeroed out on restore. In a soccer-style league with promotion/relegation, losing tie records corrupts standings and can incorrectly relegate teams.

The app already had a perfectly functional SQLite database (engine/db.py) with a saves table and JSON blob storage. Pro Leagues, FIV cycles, and season archives all used it. WVL mode simply never got wired up to it.

actions.description: Mobile tab navigation CSS: on screens <768px, tab labels hidden and only icons shown; tighter padding and no minimum width; flex-wrap: nowrap and overflow: hidden to prevent tab wrapping globally. Added .props("mobile-arrows outside-arrows") to every ui.tabs() call across 7 files.
Responsive hero banner (WVL mode): added vb-hero-banner, vb-hero-top, vb-hero-stats CSS classes; banner stacks vertically on mobile, stats row wraps and centers, padding and font sizes reduced.

WVL season serialization: added serialize_wvl_season()/deserialize_wvl_season() (delegating tiers to serialize_pro_league_season(), serializing InjuryTracker per tier, using __new__ to bypass __init__, with a fallback scanning all WVL tier directories for team JSON files), _serialize_injury_tracker()/_deserialize_injury_tracker(), and save_wvl_season()/load_wvl_season()/delete_wvl_season() using the existing save_blob()/load_blob() pattern with save_type "wvl_season".

WVL mode database migration: replaced all app.storage.user[_WVL_SEASON_KEY] reads/writes with _get_wvl_season() (cache → database → legacy storage with auto-migration), _save_wvl_season(s) (cache + database, called after every sim week, playoff advance, season start, and offseason step), and _clear_wvl_season().
Ties serialization fix: added ties and div_ties to both serialize_pro_league_season() and deserialize_pro_league_season(); old saves gracefully default to 0 via .get("ties", 0).

risk_posture: Old saves without these fields gracefully default to 0 via .get("ties", 0).
This is safe because WVL mode is single-user (one dynasty per browser session).

non_actions.item: Moving WVL dynasty storage to the database
non_actions.reason: WVLDynasty already has working to_dict()/from_dict() and is much smaller than the season object. The cookie storage works fine for it. The season was the critical piece because it contains the bulk of the data (game results, player stats, schedules across 4 tiers).

section_headers_original: Mobile Compatibility Fix and WVL State Persistence Migration
unmapped_content:
Branch: claude/fix-mobile-compatibility-bvVQV. Files Modified: main.py, nicegui_app/app.py, nicegui_app/pages/wvl_mode.py, engine/db.py, nicegui_app/pages/play.py, nicegui_app/pages/pro_leagues.py, nicegui_app/pages/international.py, nicegui_app/pages/draftyqueenz.py.

Architecture Notes — Storage layer summary after this change:
| Data | Storage | Serialization |
| WVL Dynasty | app.storage.user (JSON) | WVLDynasty.to_dict() / .from_dict() |
| WVL Season | SQLite saves table | serialize_wvl_season() / deserialize_wvl_season() |
| WVL Phase | app.storage.user (string) | Plain string, no serialization needed |
| Pro Leagues | SQLite saves table | serialize_pro_league_season() (existing) |
| FIV Cycles | SQLite saves table | Existing |

In-memory cache pattern: _wvl_season_cache is a module-level dict. This avoids hitting the database on every _get_wvl_season() call during a session (which happens frequently — dashboard, roster, schedule, and other tabs all read the season). The cache is populated on first read and updated on every write. It's cleared on season reset.

Deserialization detail: uses Injury.__dataclass_fields__ to filter out computed properties (is_season_ending, game_status) during deserialization. Dynasty data (WVLDynasty) still uses app.storage.user via to_dict()/from_dict() which was already working correctly. Only the season object needed migration. Mobile symptom example quoted: "DASHBOARDEASCHEDULPLAYOESAGUEINANGEISTO". Previously only some pages (league.py, dq_mode.py, export.py) had mobile-arrows props.
parser_notes: Date from first heading. No validation/testing section in the document.
